task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the service bulletin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit or nano) or terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/QGC/vibration_test_bulletin.txt'.
The QGroundControl application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the Parameters section in QGroundControl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Parameters' screen is active in QGroundControl, typically found under the Vehicle Setup (gear icon) menu.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configured the INS parameters according to the bulletin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGroundControl parameter list or search results showing 'INS_LOG_BAT_MASK' set to 1, 'INS_LOG_BAT_OPT' set to 0, and/or 'INS_HNTCH_ENABLE' set to 0. A parameter edit dialog for any of these may also be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configured the LOG parameters according to the bulletin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGroundControl parameter list or search results showing 'LOG_DISARMED' set to 1 and/or 'LOG_BITMASK' set to 65535. A parameter edit dialog for any of these may also be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent exported the complete parameter list to the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or export dialog in QGroundControl saving to '/home/ga/Documents/QGC/vibration_prep.params', or a file manager/terminal showing the newly created 'vibration_prep.params' file in that directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the QGroundControl GUI to modify parameters.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the QGroundControl interface, using the parameter search bar, and opening parameter edit dialogs, rather than using command-line tools to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used QGroundControl's built-in export functionality to create the .params file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the QGroundControl 'Tools' -> 'Save to file' button being clicked or the QGC file save dialog, rather than the agent manually creating a text file named 'vibration_prep.params' in a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually create the verification result file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands or scripts manually writing to '/tmp/task_result.json'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The service bulletin is located at '/home/ga/Documents/QGC/vibration_test_bulletin.txt'. The target parameters to be configured in QGroundControl are exactly: INS_LOG_BAT_MASK = 1, INS_LOG_BAT_OPT = 0, INS_HNTCH_ENABLE = 0, LOG_DISARMED = 1, and LOG_BITMASK = 65535. The final parameter list must be exported to '/home/ga/Documents/QGC/vibration_prep.params'.
